name: Event Agenda Page
description: A refined event announcement or agenda page that makes time, place, speakers, and registration easy to scan.
usage: Use for event posters, conference agendas, webinars, workshops, meetups, and invitation pages.
best_for: Event posters, conference agendas, webinars, workshops, meetups, and invitation pages.
Briefs that need hierarchy across event theme, timing, venue, speakers, and CTA.
avoid_for: Evergreen product marketing pages without a time-based program.
Long editorial articles with no schedule or attendance action.
layout_rules: Start with event theme, date, venue or online format, and registration CTA.
Use a timeline or agenda stack with time, session title, speaker, and short context.
Group speakers or highlights in a controlled grid.
Keep logistics visible near the top and repeat the CTA near the bottom.
style_rules: Use a polished event palette that matches the audience.
Use clear time labels, separators, and contrast so the schedule is easy to scan.
Use subtle motion or hover only for web reading.
content_rules: Do not invent speakers, venues, times, sponsors, or ticket details.
If details are missing, present a clean placeholder-free structure based only on known information.
Make the audience benefit and attendance action explicit.
quality_checks: Date, time, place, and CTA must be visible without hunting.
Agenda rows must not collapse into dense paragraphs.
The event identity should feel specific.
